audit_status.source_base: Greek 1 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for diaspora/exile, living hope, tested trust, prophets, holiness, imperishable seed.

verses.restored: Peter, apostle of Jesus Messiah, to chosen sojourners of dispersion in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia,
according to foreknowledge of Divine Father, in sanctification of Spirit, into obedience and sprinkling of blood of Jesus Messiah: grace to you and peace be multiplied.
Blessed be the Divine and Father of our Lord Jesus Messiah, who according to his great mercy gave us new birth into living hope through resurrection of Jesus Messiah from dead ones,
into an inheritance incorruptible and undefiled and unfading, kept in heavens for you,
who by power of Divine are guarded through trust into salvation/healing ready to be unveiled in last season.
In this you rejoice, though now for a little, if necessary, being grieved in various tests/trials,
so that the tested genuineness of your trust, more precious than gold that perishes though tested by fire, may be found into praise and glory and honor at unveiling of Jesus Messiah.
Whom not having seen, you love; in whom now not seeing yet trusting, you rejoice with joy unspeakable and glorified,
receiving the outcome of your trust, salvation/healing of souls/lives.
Concerning this salvation/healing, prophets sought and searched carefully, those prophesying about the grace toward you,
searching into what person or what season the Spirit of Messiah in them was making clear, testifying beforehand the sufferings into Messiah and the glories after these.

To them it was unveiled that not to themselves but to you they were serving these things, now announced through those bringing good news to you by holy Spirit sent from heaven, things into which messengers long to look.
Therefore, girding up the loins of your mind, being sober, hope completely upon the grace being brought to you at unveiling of Jesus Messiah.
As children of obedience, not being conformed to the former desires in your ignorance,
but according to the Holy One who called you, become holy yourselves in all conduct,
because it is written: You shall be holy, because I am holy.
And if you call upon Father the One judging impartially according to each one's work, conduct yourselves in reverent fear during the time of your sojourn,
knowing that you were released not by perishable things, silver or gold, from your futile ancestral conduct,
but by precious blood as of lamb unblemished and spotless, Messiah,
foreknown before foundation of world, but made manifest at last of the times because of you,
who through him trust in Divine, the One raising him from dead ones and giving him glory, so that your trust and hope are into Divine.
Having purified your souls/lives in obedience to truth into unhypocritical sibling-love, love one another earnestly from heart,
having been born anew not from perishable seed but imperishable, through living and remaining word of Divine.
For all flesh is like grass and all its glory like flower of grass; the grass withered and the flower fell,
but the word of Lord remains into the age. And this is the word good-newsed to you.

translation_notes: Diaspora/sojourner identity is preserved; it is not anti-Jewish but uses exile imagery for the addressed communities.
Suffering/testing is acknowledged without being glorified as an end in itself.
Hope, holiness, and sibling-love are the chapter's ethical center.
